---
# Extract IP/user/status from raw log lines, normalize, and aggregate by user

**Dataset**: `gx_09_logs`

Turns an unstructured free-text log column into structured per-user activity. Three `regextract` passes pull the IPv4 address, the `user=` value, and the `status=` code out of each raw `line`; the username is `lowercase`-normalized so `Admin` and `admin` fold together before aggregation. The final `comp` rolls up event counts plus the distinct status codes and source IPs per user, giving an analyst a fast pivot table from logs that have no native fields. Gotcha: `regextract` returns an array, so each extraction is wrapped in `arrayindex(..., 0)` to take the first capture group.

```sql
dataset = gx_09_logs
| alter ip = arrayindex(regextract(line, "(\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+)"), 0)
| alter user = lowercase(arrayindex(regextract(line, "user=(\w+)"), 0))
| alter status = arrayindex(regextract(line, "status=(\d{3})"), 0)
| comp count() as events, values(status) as statuses, values(ip) as ips by user
| sort desc events
```
